---
title: "0.1 Setup your workspace"
author: "Minh-Quan Ton-Ngoc"
date: "2026-05-11"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-expand: true
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: false
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
engine: knitr
---
```{=html}
<style>
.setup-note {
  background: #f8f9fa;
  border-left: 4px solid #0d6efd;
  padding: 1rem 1.2rem;
  border-radius: 0.4rem;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}
.check-card {
  background: #f6fff8;
  border-left: 4px solid #198754;
  padding: 1rem 1.2rem;
  border-radius: 0.4rem;
  margin: 1rem 0;
}
.manual-note {
  background: #fff8e1;
  border-left: 4px solid #f0ad4e;
  padding: 0.9rem 1rem;
  border-radius: 0.4rem;
  margin: 1rem 0;
}
.small-text {
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  color: #495057;
}
figure figcaption,
.figure-caption,
p.caption {
  text-align: center;
}
summary {
  font-weight: 600;
}
</style>
```

# Purpose

This guide helps you prepare a Windows or macOS laptop for the MicroGen 2026 workshop. It covers the tools used for coding, data analysis, and connecting to the workshop cloud server.

Most heavy bioinformatics workflows will run on the **workshop server**, not on your laptop. Your laptop mainly needs:

- a code editor,
- R and Python for small local exercises,
- SSH tools for connecting to the server,
- and optional graphical tools for users who prefer them.

::: {.callout-note}
## Required and optional tools

**Required for most participants:** Python 3.13, R 4.5.0, Visual Studio Code, the VS Code Remote - SSH extension, and the listed Python/R packages.

**Optional:** RStudio Desktop, Java, Artemis, BandageNG, SeaView, and extra X11/VNC tools. Instructors may ask you to use these for specific graphical activities.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Do not share secrets

If you receive a `.pem` private key file, do not upload it, email it, paste it into chat, or share it with another participant. The key is your login credential for the server.
:::

# Quick checklist

| Tool | Required? | Used for | Windows 10/11 | macOS Intel | macOS Apple Silicon | Ubuntu personal computer |
|---|---:|---|---|---|---|---|
| R 4.5.0 | Yes | R analysis notebooks and packages | CRAN Windows installer | CRAN `x86_64` macOS package | CRAN `arm64` macOS package | CRAN Ubuntu repository |
| RStudio Desktop | Optional | Graphical R interface | Posit installer | Posit macOS app | Posit macOS app | Posit `.deb` package if needed |
| Python 3.13 | Yes | Python exercises and checks | Python.org installer | Python.org installer or Homebrew | Python.org installer or Homebrew | Ubuntu package if available, or Python.org source |
| Visual Studio Code | Yes | Editing files and remote server work | Windows installer | macOS Intel or Universal app | macOS Apple Silicon or Universal app | Official `.deb` package |
| MobaXterm portable | Windows only | SSH, SFTP, X11, VNC tunnels | Recommended | Not used | Not used | Not used |
| Rtools45 | Sometimes | Build R packages from source if Windows package binaries are unavailable | Install only if needed | Not used | Not used | Not used |
| OpenJDK 17 | Optional | Mainly Artemis; sometimes other graphical tools | Temurin OpenJDK 17 LTS | Temurin OpenJDK 17 LTS | Temurin OpenJDK 17 LTS | `openjdk-17-jdk` |
| Artemis | Optional | Genome browsing and annotation | Java `.jar` with OpenJDK 17 | Java `.jar` with OpenJDK 17 | Java `.jar` with OpenJDK 17 | `artemis` package or Java `.jar` |
| BandageNG | Optional | Assembly graph viewing | Download release if available, or use Ubuntu/conda option | Download release if available, or use conda option | Download release if available, or use conda option | Bioconda/conda option |
| NCBI BLAST+ | Sometimes | Required for BandageNG BLAST-related features | NCBI BLAST+ installer/archive | NCBI BLAST+ archive or Homebrew | NCBI BLAST+ archive or Homebrew | `ncbi-blast+` |
| SeaView 5 | Optional | Sequence alignment and phylogeny GUI | SeaView `seaview5.exe` | SeaView macOS download | SeaView macOS download | `seaview` |
| Remote - SSH extension | Yes | Open server folders in VS Code | Install inside VS Code | Install inside VS Code | Install inside VS Code | Install inside VS Code |
| X11/VNC tools | Optional | Remote graphical applications such as FastQC | MobaXterm; optionally VcXsrv or VNC viewer | XQuartz and Screen Sharing/VNC viewer | XQuartz and Screen Sharing/VNC viewer | Native X11 desktop or VNC viewer |

<details>
<summary><strong>Click to open official download links checked for this guide</strong></summary>

These links were checked against official project pages while preparing this guide:

- R and R 4.5.0: [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/), [Windows R 4.5.0 old release](https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/base/old/4.5.0/), [macOS Intel R 4.5.0](https://cran.r-project.org/bin/macosx/big-sur-x86_64/base/), [macOS Apple Silicon R 4.5.0](https://cran.r-project.org/bin/macosx/big-sur-arm64/base/)
- RStudio Desktop: [Posit downloads](https://posit.co/downloads/)
- Python 3.13: [Python 3.13 latest maintenance release](https://www.python.org/downloads/latest/python3.13/)
- Visual Studio Code: [VS Code download page](https://code.visualstudio.com/download)
- MobaXterm portable: [MobaXterm Home Edition](https://mobaxterm.mobatek.net/download-home-edition.html)
- Rtools for Windows: [CRAN Rtools](https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/Rtools/)
- OpenJDK 17: [Eclipse Adoptium Temurin OpenJDK 17](https://adoptium.net/installation/?variant=openjdk17) or [Microsoft Build of OpenJDK 17](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)
- Artemis: [Artemis official page](https://sanger-pathogens.github.io/Artemis/Artemis/) and [GitHub releases](https://github.com/sanger-pathogens/Artemis/releases)
- BandageNG: [BandageNG GitHub](https://github.com/asl/BandageNG) and [Bioconda package](https://bioconda.github.io/recipes/bandage_ng/README.html)
- NCBI BLAST+: [NCBI BLAST+ latest downloads](https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/blast/executables/blast+/LATEST/)
- SeaView 5: [PRABI-Doua SeaView](https://doua.prabi.fr/software/seaview/)
- XQuartz: [XQuartz](https://www.xquartz.org/)

</details>

# Which instructions should I follow?

- If you use **Windows 10 or Windows 11**, follow [Windows 10/11 setup](setup-windows.qmd).
- If you use an **Intel Mac**, follow [macOS setup](setup-macos.qmd), choosing the Intel options.
- If you use an **Apple Silicon Mac** with an M1, M2, M3, M4, or newer Apple chip, follow the Apple Silicon options.
- If you use **Ubuntu on your own laptop, WSL with graphical support, or an Ubuntu virtual machine**, follow [Ubuntu setup](setup-ubuntu.qmd).

::: {.callout-tip}
## How to check your Mac chip

Open the **Apple menu** in the top-left corner, then choose **About This Mac**.

- If you see **Processor: Intel**, use the Intel Mac instructions.
- If you see **Chip: Apple M1**, **M2**, **M3**, **M4**, or similar, use the Apple Silicon instructions.
:::

![macOS About This Mac screen showing Chip or Processor](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/molepi-oucru/MicroGen-2026/main/day-0/materials/software-setup-images/macos-chip.png)
# Setup sections

Use these pages to continue the setup:

| Section | Page |
|---|---|
| Windows 10/11 setup | [Open Windows setup](setup-windows.qmd) |
| macOS setup | [Open macOS setup](setup-macos.qmd) |
| Ubuntu setup | [Open Ubuntu setup](setup-ubuntu.qmd) |
| Optional graphical bioinformatics apps | [Open bioinformatics apps setup](setup-bioinformatics-apps.qmd) |
| Workshop server access | [Open server access setup](setup-server-access.qmd) |
| Verification checklist | [Open verification checklist](setup-verification.qmd) |
| Troubleshooting and FAQs | [Open troubleshooting and FAQs](setup-troubleshooting-faq.qmd) |
| Advanced R in VS Code | [Open advanced R in VS Code](setup-r-vscode.qmd) |
